summary: Verified scroll fix for Fork·Fate result reveal. On both mobile (390x844) and desktop (1440x900), after spin the spin-result-card top settles at y=105px (just below sticky header), and the image region (top photo with restaurant name h3) is fully visible in the viewport (childTop=105, childBottom=361). No negative top / no scroll-past-image regression. Respin re-triggers with same top alignment. Shuffle popup appears during spin and dismisses on reveal. social-share row and alternatives-section still present.

issues: Card top lands at 105px vs stated ~88px target — 17px above stated offset. Image is still fully visible so no functional issue, but if the header actually renders ~88px the offset could be tightened. Non-blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: Scroll offset of 88px in Home.jsx useEffect produces effective card top of 105px — consider measuring actual header height dynamically if pixel-perfect alignment is desired, but current behavior fully solves the reported bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Primary bug (scroll past image) is FIXED. Verified on mobile 390x844 and desktop 1440x900 with ZIP 10001. cardTop=105px in both cases, restaurant image and h3 visible on-screen. Respin verified. shuffle-popup lifecycle correct.
rca of the issue: Previous scrollIntoView block:'center' centered the tall mobile result card, pushing top above viewport. New window.scrollTo({top: rectTop + scrollY - 88}) aligns card top to ~88px below viewport top, keeping the image visible.
